comment.body: i totally agree, but i rotted my brain a couple days this week watching youtube live stream "traders" and pretty much came to the conclusion that people either educate themselves or they dont... kind of like how some ppl r happy with working at mcdonalds and some are determined to become professionals.  if everybody educated themselves trading would probably become a lot  harder for us, who knows...  but i will still make fun of things when i see fit because if anything its extremely poor advertising lol. and yes totally gimmicky a well.  i just couldn't believe the math put out as a flex.  when they first pushed it too i listened to the spaces and was also unimpressed but those numbers blew my mind lol

comment.body:
this is why i didn't create discord.
i worked and work for serious big companies on their projects, and both in the world of business and in the world of politics, i learned that no matter wo you are and what you are doing, but the way you are doing it and the ppl you are doing it is what will judge you.
everything is a symbol and so has a symbolic meaning. and that meaning just sticks to your project.

discord is associated mostly with furus, signal alerts, mediocre money-grubber ppl + gamers, 67s etc. 
while substack is associated with publicists, researchers publicing their thouhgts in their free time... even if the chat is horrible (bcs it is not a chat, it is a comment thread, originially)

so i must have chosen what i wanna be associated with.
here it feels more sophisticated
